Greater Seattle: Daycare & Childcare Access vs. Household Income

Spatial analysis across King, Snohomish & Pierce Counties

ACS 5-Year Estimates — 2022 OSM: amenity=kindergarten / childcare Census TIGER Tracts

Avg Daycares / Tract
-0.014
Income–Daycare Corr (r)
Pearson r = -0.0141 — this measures the linear relationship between median household income and the number of daycare/childcare facilities per census tract. A near-zero r suggests daycare count is spread fairly evenly across income levels, though quantity alone does not capture affordability or quality disparities.

Choropleth Maps

Left: Median household income by census tract (green = higher income). Navy dots mark daycare and childcare locations. Higher-income tracts concentrate in the Eastside (Bellevue, Kirkland, Redmond) and North Seattle suburbs.

Right: Daycare/childcare count per census tract. Clusters in denser urban tracts may reflect both population density and commercial zoning rather than income alone.

Income vs. Daycare Count — Scatter Plot

Each point is a census tract. The OLS line shows the direction of the relationship. Outlier tracts with many daycares at any income level are typically dense commercial or mixed-use zones. Note that OSM coverage may be incomplete in some suburban areas.

Q40.480.082172
Q5 (highest)0.510.088172
The quintile table reveals whether childcare facilities cluster in richer or poorer tracts. Even if counts are similar, affordability (subsidy availability, sliding-scale fees) and quality (licensed capacity, staff ratios) tend to differ significantly by neighborhood income — factors not captured by location counts alone.

Limitations & Next Steps

LimitationSuggested improvement
OSM coverage is crowd-sourced and may miss unlicensed or home-based daycares Merge with WA DCYF licensed childcare facility list
School count treats all tracts equally regardless of population or children under 5 Normalize by tract population aged 0–4 (ACS table B01001)
Raw count ignores capacity, cost, and operating hours Add DCYF licensed capacity data for a weighted access score
Spatial join assigns daycares to tracts; border facilities may serve adjacent tracts Use distance-based catchment areas or travel-time isochrones

Data Sources & Methods

DatasetSourceDetail
Census Tract BoundariesCensus TIGER/Line 2022 WA State — King, Snohomish, Pierce counties
Median Household IncomeACS 5-Year Estimates 2022 Table B19013_001E via Census API
Daycare / Childcare LocationsOpenStreetMap via Overpass API amenity=kindergarten & amenity=childcare, bbox 47.0–48.2°N, 121.4–123.0°W
AnalysisPython: geopandas, pandas, matplotlib Spatial join (point-in-polygon), Pearson correlation, OLS regression
Generated with Python • geopandas • matplotlib • Census ACS API • OpenStreetMap
